message:
fix(dashboard): prevent memory leak in log history rotation

Add periodic compaction when the backing array capacity exceeds 2x the
needed size. The slice-reslicing rotation pattern (lines = lines[1:])
keeps references to old LogLine structs in the backing array, causing
unbounded memory growth over time with 10,000+ lines rotating.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -230,6 +230,10 @@ EOF
 # Enable systemd-networkd
 RUN systemctl enable systemd-networkd 2>/dev/null || true
 
+# Disable systemd-resolved (causes boot delays and DNS issues in Firecracker)
+# resolv.conf is set up by stockyard-init.sh at boot time
+RUN systemctl disable systemd-resolved 2>/dev/null || true
+
 # ============================================================================
 # Section 8: MicroVM Kernel
 # ============================================================================

@@ -25,6 +25,11 @@ for i in {1..30}; do
     sleep 1
 done
 
+# Set up DNS (systemd-resolved is disabled)
+echo "Configuring DNS..."
+rm -f /etc/resolv.conf 2>/dev/null || true
+printf 'nameserver 8.8.8.8\nnameserver 8.8.4.4\n' > /etc/resolv.conf
+
 # Ensure MMDS route exists (systemd-networkd should add it, but verify)
 if ! ip route | grep -q "169.254.169.254"; then
     echo "Adding MMDS route..."
